summary: Exceptionally well-designed for its purpose. The 3-file architecture (prepare.py immutable + train.py mutable + program.md instructions) is a deliberate constraint that makes autonomous experimentation tractable. Code quality is high — clean PyTorch, proper dataclasses, clear separation between fixed infrastructure and experimental code. The program.md is a masterclass in AI agent instruction design.

findings.detail: Perfect separation: prepare.py (immutable infrastructure), train.py (experiment target), program.md (agent instructions). Each file has one clear responsibility.

Clean modern Python. Dataclasses for config (GPTConfig). Type hints on key functions. Well-structured PyTorch model with clear forward passes. Some long functions (train loop ~100 lines) but appropriate for the domain.

program.md is an exceptional skill file: clear setup protocol, explicit constraints (what agent CAN and CANNOT do), experiment loop with keep/discard logic, TSV logging format, crash recovery, NEVER STOP instruction. This is the most notable extractable pattern.
TSV-based results logging (commit, val_bpb, memory, status, description). Simple but effective. Git branch per experiment run. No MLflow/W&B integration — deliberately minimal.

NaN detection in training loop with fast-fail. Crash recovery instructions in program.md. Download retries in prepare.py. No structured error handling in train.py itself (crashes are expected and handled by the agent).
Not designed for extension. Single GPU, single file, single metric. Forks handle platform variations (MPS, ROCm). This is intentional — complexity budget is zero.
train.py: 630 LOC, prepare.py: 389 LOC. Total 1,019 LOC. Impressive for a complete GPT training pipeline with tokenizer, data loading, and evaluation.
